poeticGestures.title: Traditional Poetic Gesture-Images (手勢圖 shǒushì tú)
poeticGestures.intro: Classical handbooks pair each finger technique with a poetic nature image and a four-character verse introduced by "興曰 Xìng yuē". The Taiyin Daquanji set has 33 illustrations: 17 right-hand, then 16 left-hand. These examples are representative; the full set is on the source page.

examples.image: Lone duck looks back at the flock (孤鶩顧群)
Behold the howling ape, climbing the tall tree; wishing to go up yet not up, its momentum pressing on (瞻彼號猿，升於喬木。欲上不上，其勢逐逐)
Likened to a cicada singing in autumn

glyphFontResources.name: Adrakaris/guqin-jianzipu-font
glyphFontResources.note: Noto Sans SC packed with ligatures for writing guqin score notation.

glyphFontResources.name: alephpi/jianzipu
glyphFontResources.note: Jianzipu input, encoding and display solution (Python parser + OpenType font).

glyphFontResources.name: neuralfirings/JianZiPu
glyphFontResources.note: A font for writing guqin music in jianzipu via glyph-layering ligatures.

glyphFontResources.name: wds-seu/guqinMM
glyphFontResources.note: Academic OCR + dataset (MIT-licensed code); jianzipu recognition models on a 五声琴谱-based dataset.

glyphFontResources.name: Guqin NLTabs (web notation tool)
glyphFontResources.note: Renders jianzipu; docs enumerate the shorthand-to-jianzipu mapping.

licensing: Pei-You (Judy) Chang (peiyouqin.com): instructions, images, and demonstration videos are her copyrighted work (© Judy (Pei-You) Chang). For an app, link to her pages or request licensing — do not redistribute her videos/images without permission.

John Thompson / silkqin.com: translations and photographic reproductions of the Taiyin Daquanji gesture-images are his work. The underlying woodblock images are historical (likely public domain), but his scans/translations are not — request permission or link out.

The technique instructions in this reference are paraphrased/summarized for spec purposes; the wording here may be used freely, but the original videos and images must be sourced and cleared as above.
The Chinese-language enthusiast pages (guoqinwang, Zhihu) are convenient references but of uncertain rights — treat as background, not shippable assets.

Bundled fonts (assets/fonts/): Qin Jianzipu (qinfont.woff2; jianzipu designs © Yijun Hu, based on Noto Sans SC; from Adrakaris/guqin-jianzipu-font) and a subset of Noto Serif SC are both under the SIL Open Font License 1.1 — see assets/fonts/OFL.txt. They may be redistributed with this software but not sold on their own.
coverageNote: Sections A–G cover roughly 60 named techniques — the working vocabulary of modern practice. Historical handbooks document on the order of 1,000+ finger-technique glyphs (most obsolete). For completeness on historical tablature, expand from the silkqin Taiyin Daquanji folios and Zha Fuxi's reference Cunjian Guqin Zhifa Puzi Jilan. For a learner-facing product, A–G plus the poetic images is the right scope.
